risk_summary: Moderate exposure. The URL shows enough surface area that it should be reviewed before being treated as hardened.

findings.title: Insecure transport
findings.detail: The URL does not use HTTPS, so credentials, cookies, and content can be exposed or altered in transit.

findings.title: Missing protective response headers
findings.detail: Several baseline browser hardening headers are absent.

attack_families.why_it_matters: Insecure transport makes credential capture and traffic manipulation easier for attackers on-path.
attack_families.common_attacker_behavior: Attackers look for plain HTTP, weak redirects, and downgrade opportunities.

attack_families.why_it_matters: Weak response headers make client-side abuse, framing, and content confusion easier.
attack_families.common_attacker_behavior: Attackers combine weak headers with phishing pages, script injection, or deceptive embedding.

countermeasures: Serve the site over HTTPS only and redirect plain HTTP permanently.
Enable HSTS once TLS is stable so browsers stop attempting insecure transport.
Set a strict Content-Security-Policy and frame protections.
Send X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, and HSTS consistently.
